Home / Sem 5 / INT364 / Unit 3 MCQ
Back to Subject
View Notes

Unit 3 - Practice Quiz

INT364 50 Questions
0 Correct 0 Wrong 50 Left
0/50

1 What is the primary definition of Amazon S3?

A. A file storage service
B. A relational database service
C. A block storage service
D. An object storage service

Correct Answer: An object storage service

Explanation:

Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) is an object storage service that offers industry-leading scalability, data availability, security, and performance.

Incorrect! Try again.

2 Which Amazon S3 storage class is best suited for data that is accessed frequently and requires low latency?

A. S3 Glacier
B. S3 One Zone-IA
C. S3 Standard
D. S3 Standard-IA

Correct Answer: S3 Standard

Explanation:

S3 Standard offers high durability, availability, and performance for frequently accessed data.

Incorrect! Try again.

3 What is the durability guarantee provided by the Amazon S3 Standard storage class?

A. 99.999999999% (11 9s)
B. 99.0%
C. 99.99%
D. 99.9%

Correct Answer: 99.999999999% (11 9s)

Explanation:

Amazon S3 is designed to provide 99.999999999% (11 9s) of data durability for objects over a given year.

Incorrect! Try again.

4 Which S3 storage class automatically moves objects between two access tiers based on changing access patterns?

A. S3 Standard-IA
B. S3 Glacier Deep Archive
C. S3 Standard
D. S3 Intelligent-Tiering

Correct Answer: S3 Intelligent-Tiering

Explanation:

S3 Intelligent-Tiering is designed to optimize costs by automatically moving data to the most cost-effective access tier, without performance impact or operational overhead.

Incorrect! Try again.

5 In Amazon S3, what is the maximum size of a single object?

A. 5 TB
B. 1 TB
C. 5 GB
D. 100 GB

Correct Answer: 5 TB

Explanation:

The maximum size of a single object that can be uploaded to Amazon S3 is 5 TB.

Incorrect! Try again.

6 Which S3 storage class stores data in a single Availability Zone and is less expensive than Standard-IA?

A. S3 Standard
B. S3 Glacier
C. S3 One Zone-IA
D. S3 Intelligent-Tiering

Correct Answer: S3 One Zone-IA

Explanation:

S3 One Zone-IA stores data in a single AZ, making it cost-effective for data that can be recreated if the AZ fails.

Incorrect! Try again.

7 What is the primary use case for S3 Glacier Deep Archive?

A. Storing session data for web applications
B. Long-term data retention (10+ years) accessed rarely
C. Frequently accessed database backups
D. Hosting static websites

Correct Answer: Long-term data retention (10+ years) accessed rarely

Explanation:

S3 Glacier Deep Archive is the lowest-cost storage class and supports long-term retention and digital preservation for data that may be accessed once or twice in a year.

Incorrect! Try again.

8 To enable faster file uploads to an S3 bucket from geographically dispersed users, which feature should be used?

A. S3 Select
B. S3 Lifecycle Policies
C. S3 Versioning
D. S3 Transfer Acceleration

Correct Answer: S3 Transfer Acceleration

Explanation:

S3 Transfer Acceleration enables fast, easy, and secure transfers of files over long distances between your client and an S3 bucket by utilizing CloudFront's edge locations.

Incorrect! Try again.

9 What requirement must an S3 bucket name fulfill?

A. It must be unique within the user's account only
B. It must be unique across all AWS accounts globally
C. It must be unique within the region
D. It can contain uppercase letters

Correct Answer: It must be unique across all AWS accounts globally

Explanation:

S3 bucket names are globally unique across all AWS accounts; once a name is taken by any user, it cannot be used by another.

Incorrect! Try again.

10 Which S3 feature allows you to define rules to automatically transition objects to cheaper storage classes or expire them?

A. S3 Object Lock
B. Cross-Region Replication
C. S3 Lifecycle Policies
D. S3 Versioning

Correct Answer: S3 Lifecycle Policies

Explanation:

Lifecycle configurations allow you to specify rules to transition objects to another storage class or expire (delete) them after a specific period.

Incorrect! Try again.

11 When S3 Versioning is enabled, what happens if you upload a file with the same key (name) as an existing file?

A. The upload fails
B. The existing file is overwritten permanently
C. The old file is moved to Glacier
D. Amazon S3 generates a new version ID for the uploaded object

Correct Answer: Amazon S3 generates a new version ID for the uploaded object

Explanation:

With versioning enabled, S3 retains the existing object and adds the new object with a unique version ID, allowing retrieval of previous versions.

Incorrect! Try again.

12 What object is created when you delete a file in a versioning-enabled S3 bucket without specifying a version ID?

A. A delete marker
B. A null version
C. An archived copy
D. A snapshot

Correct Answer: A delete marker

Explanation:

When you delete an object in a versioned bucket, S3 inserts a delete marker, which becomes the current version of the object, effectively hiding the object without permanently removing previous versions.

Incorrect! Try again.

13 Which feature creates a physical layer of security by requiring a second form of authentication to permanently delete an object version?

A. MFA Delete
B. Bucket Policy
C. IAM Role
D. S3 Object Lock

Correct Answer: MFA Delete

Explanation:

MFA Delete requires additional authentication (Multi-Factor Authentication) to change the versioning state of a bucket or permanently delete an object version.

Incorrect! Try again.

14 What is an Amazon Machine Image (AMI)?

A. A virtual network definition
B. A storage volume attached to an instance
C. A physical server in a data center
D. A template that contains the software configuration (OS, application server, etc.) required to launch an instance

Correct Answer: A template that contains the software configuration (OS, application server, etc.) required to launch an instance

Explanation:

An AMI is a template that provides the information required to launch an EC2 instance, including the OS and pre-installed software.

Incorrect! Try again.

15 Which category of AMIs is generally considered the safest and most reliable to use?

A. Private AMIs shared by unknown accounts
B. AWS Marketplace AMIs (Verified providers)
C. My AMIs
D. Community AMIs

Correct Answer: AWS Marketplace AMIs (Verified providers)

Explanation:

AWS Marketplace AMIs and Quick Start AMIs provided by AWS are verified and tested, whereas Community AMIs may carry security risks if the source is untrusted.

Incorrect! Try again.

16 Which EC2 instance family is optimized for compute-bound applications like high-performance web servers and batch processing?

A. C-series (Compute Optimized)
B. T-series (General Purpose)
C. I-series (Storage Optimized)
D. R-series (Memory Optimized)

Correct Answer: C-series (Compute Optimized)

Explanation:

The C-series instances are Compute Optimized, delivering high performance processors for compute-intensive workloads.

Incorrect! Try again.

17 Which EC2 instance type is ideal for applications that need high memory performance, such as in-memory databases?

A. Memory Optimized
B. Compute Optimized
C. General Purpose
D. Accelerated Computing

Correct Answer: Memory Optimized

Explanation:

Memory Optimized instances are designed to deliver fast performance for workloads that process large data sets in memory.

Incorrect! Try again.

18 What is the primary characteristic of EC2 Spot Instances?

A. They are reserved for a 1 or 3-year term
B. They provide dedicated physical hardware
C. They offer unused EC2 capacity at a steep discount but can be interrupted
D. They are always the most expensive option

Correct Answer: They offer unused EC2 capacity at a steep discount but can be interrupted

Explanation:

Spot Instances allow you to request unused EC2 instances at steep discounts, but AWS can reclaim them with a 2-minute warning if capacity is needed.

Incorrect! Try again.

19 What is 'User Data' in the context of launching an EC2 instance?

A. Data stored in the /home directory
B. Scripts or commands entered during launch that run when the instance first boots
C. The metadata tags attached to the instance
D. The login credentials for the instance

Correct Answer: Scripts or commands entered during launch that run when the instance first boots

Explanation:

User data allows you to specify a script (e.g., shell script or cloud-init directive) that runs only once during the first boot cycle of the instance.

Incorrect! Try again.

20 What is the special IP address used to retrieve EC2 Instance Metadata from within the instance?

A. 10.0.0.1
B. 169.254.169.254
C. 192.168.1.1
D. 127.0.0.1

Correct Answer: 169.254.169.254

Explanation:

169.254.169.254 is the link-local address used to access instance metadata (data about your instance) and user data.

Incorrect! Try again.

21 Which storage option is block-level storage that behaves like a physical hard drive attached to an EC2 instance?

A. Amazon EBS (Elastic Block Store)
B. Amazon Glacier
C. Amazon EFS (Elastic File System)
D. Amazon S3

Correct Answer: Amazon EBS (Elastic Block Store)

Explanation:

EBS provides block-level storage volumes for use with EC2 instances, behaving like a raw hard drive that can be formatted and mounted.

Incorrect! Try again.

22 What is a key availability constraint of an Amazon EBS volume?

A. It can be attached to instances in any region
B. It is locked to a specific Availability Zone
C. It is automatically replicated across all zones in a region
D. It can be accessed over the public internet directly

Correct Answer: It is locked to a specific Availability Zone

Explanation:

EBS volumes are created in a specific Availability Zone and can only be attached to instances in that same Availability Zone.

Incorrect! Try again.

23 If you terminate an EC2 instance, what happens to the attached EBS root volume by default?

A. It is preserved
B. It is deleted
C. It is archived to Glacier
D. It is converted to a snapshot

Correct Answer: It is deleted

Explanation:

By default, the 'DeleteOnTermination' attribute for the root EBS volume is set to true, meaning the volume is deleted when the instance terminates. This setting can be changed.

Incorrect! Try again.

24 Which EBS volume type is best for mission-critical low-latency or high-throughput workloads requiring sustained IOPS?

A. General Purpose SSD (gp2/gp3)
B. Provisioned IOPS SSD (io1/io2)
C. Cold HDD (sc1)
D. Throughput Optimized HDD (st1)

Correct Answer: Provisioned IOPS SSD (io1/io2)

Explanation:

Provisioned IOPS SSD volumes are designed to meet the needs of I/O-intensive workloads that require low latency and consistent performance.

Incorrect! Try again.

25 How can you back up an EBS volume?

A. By taking a Snapshot
B. By using S3 Object Lock
C. By converting it to an AMI
D. By copying the data to RAM

Correct Answer: By taking a Snapshot

Explanation:

You can back up the data on your EBS volumes to Amazon S3 by taking point-in-time snapshots.

Incorrect! Try again.

26 Which storage service is a fully managed file system that supports the Network File System (NFS) protocol and allows concurrent access from multiple EC2 instances?

A. Amazon S3
B. Amazon EFS
C. Amazon RDS
D. Amazon EBS

Correct Answer: Amazon EFS

Explanation:

Amazon EFS provides a simple, scalable, fully managed elastic NFS file system for use with AWS Cloud services and on-premises resources.

Incorrect! Try again.

27 Unlike EBS, how does Amazon EFS handle storage capacity?

A. It requires manual resizing via the console
B. It is limited to 1 TB
C. You must provision a fixed size
D. It shrinks and grows automatically as you add/remove files

Correct Answer: It shrinks and grows automatically as you add/remove files

Explanation:

Amazon EFS is elastic; it automatically grows and shrinks as you add and remove files, so you don't need to provision storage in advance.

Incorrect! Try again.

28 Regarding the Well-Architected Framework: Which pillar focuses on selecting the right instance type for the workload?

A. Operational Excellence
B. Performance Efficiency
C. Reliability
D. Security

Correct Answer: Performance Efficiency

Explanation:

Performance Efficiency includes the ability to use computing resources efficiently to meet system requirements, such as selecting the right resource types and sizes.

Incorrect! Try again.

29 How does using Spot Instances contribute to the 'Cost Optimization' pillar of the Well-Architected Framework?

A. They ensure 100% availability
B. They provide the highest performance
C. They reduce costs for fault-tolerant, flexible workloads
D. They eliminate the need for storage

Correct Answer: They reduce costs for fault-tolerant, flexible workloads

Explanation:

Spot Instances allow users to utilize spare capacity at significantly reduced rates, optimizing costs for workloads that can handle interruptions.

Incorrect! Try again.

30 Which security measure applies to the 'Security' pillar when managing EC2 instances?

A. Using the largest instance type available
B. Turning off CloudWatch logs
C. Using IAM roles to grant permissions instead of hardcoding credentials
D. Making all security groups open to 0.0.0.0/0

Correct Answer: Using IAM roles to grant permissions instead of hardcoding credentials

Explanation:

A key security best practice is to attach IAM roles to instances to manage credentials securely rather than embedding access keys in code.

Incorrect! Try again.

31 In the context of Reliability, what is the benefit of using an Auto Scaling Group with EC2?

A. It serves static content to users
B. It ensures the correct number of instances are running to handle load and recover from failures
C. It automatically encrypts data
D. It reduces the latency of S3 transfers

Correct Answer: It ensures the correct number of instances are running to handle load and recover from failures

Explanation:

Auto Scaling helps maintain application availability and reliability by adding or removing instances based on conditions you define.

Incorrect! Try again.

32 What is the maximum file size supported by Amazon S3?

A. 5 TB
B. 1 TB
C. Unlimited
D. 10 TB

Correct Answer: 5 TB

Explanation:

The largest object that can be uploaded to S3 in a single PUT is 5 GB, but using multipart upload, the maximum object size is 5 TB.

Incorrect! Try again.

33 Which tool can be used to physically migrate petabytes of data to Amazon S3 when internet transfer is too slow?

A. AWS Snowball
B. Amazon Kinesis
C. AWS Direct Connect
D. S3 Transfer Acceleration

Correct Answer: AWS Snowball

Explanation:

AWS Snowball is a physical data transport solution that uses secure devices to transfer large amounts of data into and out of the AWS Cloud.

Incorrect! Try again.

34 A 'T3' EC2 instance utilizes what type of performance characteristic?

A. Fixed high performance
B. Burstable performance
C. Storage optimization
D. GPU acceleration

Correct Answer: Burstable performance

Explanation:

T-series instances are 'burstable' performance instances that provide a baseline level of CPU performance with the ability to burst above the baseline.

Incorrect! Try again.

35 Which EC2 purchasing option allows you to reserve capacity for a 1 or 3-year term in exchange for a significant discount?

A. On-Demand
B. Spot Instances
C. Dedicated Hosts
D. Reserved Instances

Correct Answer: Reserved Instances

Explanation:

Reserved Instances provide a significant discount (up to 75%) compared to On-Demand pricing and provide a capacity reservation when used in a specific AZ.

Incorrect! Try again.

36 To secure login access to a Linux EC2 instance, what must be created and downloaded during launch?

A. An IAM Policy
B. A password file
C. A Security Group
D. A Key Pair (.pem file)

Correct Answer: A Key Pair (.pem file)

Explanation:

Amazon EC2 uses public-key cryptography to encrypt and decrypt login information. You use the private key (.pem) to SSH into Linux instances.

Incorrect! Try again.

37 Which storage service is region-resilient (stored across multiple AZs) by default?

A. Amazon S3 Standard
B. Instance Store
C. EBS Snapshots (prior to 2019)
D. Amazon EBS

Correct Answer: Amazon S3 Standard

Explanation:

Amazon S3 Standard storage class redundantly stores data across a minimum of three Availability Zones within a region.

Incorrect! Try again.

38 What is 'Instance Store' (Ephemeral Storage)?

A. Temporary block-level storage located on disks physically attached to the host computer
B. A database service
C. Permanent block storage
D. A type of S3 bucket

Correct Answer: Temporary block-level storage located on disks physically attached to the host computer

Explanation:

Instance Store provides temporary block-level storage. Data on an instance store volume persists only during the life of the associated instance; if the instance is stopped or terminated, the data is lost.

Incorrect! Try again.

39 EBS Multi-Attach allows you to attach a single io1/io2 volume to:

A. Instances and S3 buckets simultaneously
B. Multiple instances in the same Availability Zone
C. Multiple instances in different Regions
D. An infinite number of instances

Correct Answer: Multiple instances in the same Availability Zone

Explanation:

EBS Multi-Attach enables you to attach a single Provisioned IOPS SSD (io1 or io2) volume to multiple instances that are in the same Availability Zone.

Incorrect! Try again.

40 Which S3 feature allows you to use SQL expressions to retrieve only a subset of data from an object?

A. S3 Intelligent-Tiering
B. S3 Inventory
C. S3 Select
D. S3 Glacier

Correct Answer: S3 Select

Explanation:

S3 Select enables applications to retrieve only a subset of data from an object by using simple SQL expressions, improving performance and reducing cost.

Incorrect! Try again.

41 If you need a static IP address for your EC2 instance that persists even if the instance is stopped and restarted, what should you use?

A. Elastic IP
B. Dynamic DNS
C. Private IP
D. Public IP

Correct Answer: Elastic IP

Explanation:

An Elastic IP address is a static IPv4 address designed for dynamic cloud computing. It remains associated with your account until you release it.

Incorrect! Try again.

42 Which of the following is NOT a valid state for an S3 Lifecycle policy rule?

A. Transition to Glacier
B. Transition to EBS
C. Transition to Standard-IA
D. Expire (Delete)

Correct Answer: Transition to EBS

Explanation:

You cannot transition S3 objects to EBS volumes. EBS and S3 are fundamentally different storage types (Block vs Object).

Incorrect! Try again.

43 In the context of EC2, what acts as a virtual firewall to control inbound and outbound traffic?

A. Network ACL
B. IAM Role
C. Route Table
D. Security Group

Correct Answer: Security Group

Explanation:

A Security Group acts as a virtual firewall for your EC2 instances to control incoming and outgoing traffic.

Incorrect! Try again.

44 When launching an EC2 instance, which configuration determines the network location (subnet) of the instance?

A. Instance type selection
B. AMI selection
C. Storage configuration
D. Network settings / VPC configuration

Correct Answer: Network settings / VPC configuration

Explanation:

During launch, you select the VPC and the specific Subnet (which maps to an AZ) in the Network settings.

Incorrect! Try again.

45 Which storage class has the longest retrieval time (ranging from 12 to 48 hours)?

A. S3 Standard
B. S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval
C. S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval
D. S3 Glacier Deep Archive

Correct Answer: S3 Glacier Deep Archive

Explanation:

S3 Glacier Deep Archive has the longest retrieval times, typically within 12 hours for standard retrieval and up to 48 hours for bulk retrieval.

Incorrect! Try again.

46 For a High Performance Computing (HPC) workload requiring low network latency between instances, which placement group strategy should be used?

A. Partition
B. Cluster
C. Regional
D. Spread

Correct Answer: Cluster

Explanation:

A Cluster placement group packs instances close together inside an Availability Zone to achieve low latency and high network throughput.

Incorrect! Try again.

47 What is the main difference between stopping and terminating an EC2 instance?

A. There is no difference
B. Stopping keeps the EBS volume and instance ID; terminating deletes the instance permanently
C. Stopping deletes the instance; terminating saves it
D. Terminating pauses billing; stopping continues billing

Correct Answer: Stopping keeps the EBS volume and instance ID; terminating deletes the instance permanently

Explanation:

Stopping an instance shuts it down but keeps the configuration and EBS volumes (you can restart it). Terminating deletes the instance permanently.

Incorrect! Try again.

48 Which S3 functionality allows you to host a client-side Single Page Application (SPA)?

A. Requester Pays
B. Static Website Hosting
C. S3 Transfer Acceleration
D. BitTorrent Support

Correct Answer: Static Website Hosting

Explanation:

S3 can be configured for Static Website Hosting, allowing it to serve HTML, CSS, and JS files as a website.

Incorrect! Try again.

49 If you want to ensure an EC2 instance runs on physical hardware dedicated to your use (for compliance or licensing), what option do you choose?

A. Reserved Instance
B. On-Demand Instance
C. Spot Instance
D. Dedicated Host

Correct Answer: Dedicated Host

Explanation:

A Dedicated Host is a physical server with EC2 instance capacity fully dedicated to your use, often used for compliance or BYOL (Bring Your Own License) scenarios.

Incorrect! Try again.

50 Applying the 'Operational Excellence' pillar to storage, what is a best practice?

A. Using the most expensive storage tier to ensure safety
B. Never monitoring storage usage
C. Manually deleting old files
D. Using Infrastructure as Code (IaC) to provision storage resources

Correct Answer: Using Infrastructure as Code (IaC) to provision storage resources

Explanation:

Operational Excellence emphasizes performing operations as code (IaC) to ensure consistency, reduce errors, and enable easy replication of infrastructure.

Incorrect! Try again.

Previous MCQ
Unit 2
Next MCQ
Unit 4
